en.name: N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) is the inactive 76-amino-acid N-terminal fragment of proBNP, co-secreted with BNP by ventricular cardiomyocytes in response to myocardial wall stretch. Like BNP, plasma NT-proBNP rises in heart failure; it is widely used to rule out heart failure in patients presenting with dyspnoea, and to assess prognosis and treatment response [1, 2]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by immunoassay. Results are reported as a mass concentration in pg/mL (or ng/L — numerically identical) [3]. **Note: NT-proBNP vs BNP.** BNP (active, short half-life ~ 20 min) and NT-proBNP (inactive, longer half-life ~ 60-120 min) are produced in equimolar amounts, but their plasma concentrations differ by roughly an order of magnitude (NT-proBNP higher). The two are **not numerically interchangeable** and require different cut-offs [1]. NT-proBNP is more sensitive to reduced renal clearance (rises with falling eGFR) and to obesity (lower in higher BMI). This concept set covers **NT-proBNP only**. BNP is captured by the separate concept set "B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) in blood". ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on three LOINC Hierarchy concepts (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), each expanded to include all their descendants. The set resolves to five LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma - *3029187 / 33762-6 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *42529224 / 83107-3 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay (LOINC)* - *3029435 / 33763-4 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: SI form. - *42529225 / 83108-1 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay (LOINC)*: SI form, immunoassay. ### Whole blood - *42870364 / 71425-3 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Blood by Immunoassay (LOINC)* - *1469712 / 106735-4 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by Rapid immunoassay (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`pg/mL`** (concept 8806 — picogram per millilitre) for `[Mass/volume]` codes and **`pmol/L`** (concept 9555 — picomole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes. `1 pg/mL = 1 ng/L` (numerically equal). Conversion to pmol/L: `1 pg/mL ≈ 0.118 pmol/L` (molar mass of NT-proBNP = 8.5 kDa). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits: - **Pleural fluid, peritoneal dialysis fluid specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports an NT-proBNP value: `3029187 / 33762-6 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. - **Source reports BNP**: do **not** map. Use "B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) in blood" instead. - **Do not confuse with**: BNP (separate concept set, different molecule and cut-offs), MR-proANP (mid-regional pro-atrial natriuretic peptide). ## References [1] Mueller C, McDonald K, de Boer RA, Maisel A, Cleland JGF, Kozhuharov N, et al. Heart Failure Association of the European Society of Cardiology practical guidance on the use of natriuretic peptide concentrations. *Eur J Heart Fail*. 2019;21(6):715-31. Available: https://doi.org/10.1002/ejhf.1494 [2] McDonagh TA, Metra M, Adamo M, Gardner RS, Baumbach A, Böhm M, et al. 2021 ESC Guidelines for the diagnosis and treatment of acute and chronic heart failure. *Eur Heart J*. 2021;42(36):3599-3726. Available: https://doi.org/10.1093/eurheartj/ehab368 [3] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 33762-6 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/33762-6/
